routing.guidance: Classifies the problem as a company, product, or go-to-market system.

boundary.guidance: Pulls in buyer, user, workflow, channel, ecosystem, and execution context.

structure.guidance: Makes PMF, switching cost, trust, onboarding friction, and channel behavior central structural elements.

scenarios.guidance: Favors adoption, stall, competitor displacement, channel leverage, and execution-miss branches.

question_generation.guidance: Favors questions on buyer behavior, distribution, adoption bottlenecks, pricing, and retention quality.

evidence_planning.guidance: Prefers operating metrics, customer behavior, workflow evidence, and field friction evidence.

decision_logic.guidance: Shapes what action means: resource allocation, sequencing, investment, pricing, or GTM change.

signal_translation.guidance: Signals should emphasize churn, activation, win rates, adoption depth, and time-to-value.

monitoring.guidance: Monitoring should track segment behavior, usage quality, feature adoption, and sales-cycle change.

render.guidance: Mainly affects examples and operational vocabulary.
